Name: AAI SPORTS SCHOLARSHIP SCHEME FOR OUTSTANDING SPORTSPERSONS 2025-26
Award: Up to ₹20,000 per month and other benefits
Eligibility: Sportspersons between 14 to 24 years age group
About: The AAI Sports Scholarship Scheme for Outstanding Sportspersons 2025-26 is an initiative by the Airport Authority of India (AAI) to provide scholarships to sportspersons between 14 to 24 years age group. This scheme enhances the standards of sports at the Airport Authority of India (AAI) and also encourages young talent. The selected candidates will be awarded up to ₹20,000.
Eligibility_Detail: Sportspersons between 14 to 24 years age group
Benefits: The selected candidates will receive various benefits mentioned below:  Age	Scholarship Amount Over 14 and under 18 years	₹15,000 Above 18 years 	₹20,000  Note:- The duration of the AAI Sports Contract Scheme will be one year.
Documents: Recent passport-size photo Age proof  Sports certificates
How_to_Apply: Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button. Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID) Step 3: Click on the notification ‘INDUCTION OF MERITORIOUS SPORTSPERSONS UNDER AAI SPORTS CONTRACT & SCHOLARSHIP SCHEME 2025-26’ link.  Step 4: Print out the application form.  Step 5: Post it to the concerned authority and the address listed below.   To: Shri G.L. Verma, General Manager (HR) & Secretary, AAI - Sports Control Board, Address: Airports Authority of India, Rajiv Gandhi Bhawan, 'A' Block, Room No. C-109, Safdarjung Airport, New Delhi-110 003, Phone number - 24632950
Important_Dates: Application Deadline: 30th April 2025
Selection_Criteria: The students will be selected based on the fulfilment of the eligibility criteria.
Terms_and_Conditions: The selected sportsperson is not eligible to receive any other financial assistance from any other source for sporting activities during the contract period. The sportsperson must represent only the AAI Team in national/international tournaments during their tenure. Participation in any other tournament requires a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the Secretary, AAI-SCB. Violation of the NOC condition will lead to full recovery of the amount paid by AAI-SCB and debarment from future selection. There is no obligation on AAI to offer regular employment to the selected contract sportsperson. The sportsperson must follow all AAI rules, regulations, and instructions regarding practice timings, location, conduct, attendance, and expense reimbursement. The sportsperson must put in their best efforts and maintain the image and reputation of AAI One must continue academics with the concerned sports diligently and must show growth in both areas, with the submission of self-attested copies of documents by the head of the institution they are in.
Contact_Details: Airports Authority of India, Rajiv Gandhi Bhawan, 'A' Block, Room No. C-109, Safdarjung Airport, New Delhi-110 003 Phone Number: 24632950  Source: https://www.aai.aero/en/
Important_Links: Apply online link Original website Latest scholarship link
FAQs: Question: What is the AAI Sports Scholarship Scheme for Outstanding Sportspersons 2025-26? Answer: The AAI Sports Scholarship Scheme is an initiative by the Sports Control Board of the Airports Authority of India (AAI) to provide financial assistance to promising young Indian sportspersons who have demonstrated excellence in their respective sports at the national level.   Question: Who is eligible to apply for the AAI Sports Scholarship 2025-26? Answer: To be eligible, applicants must be Indian citizens aged between 14 and 24 years as of March 31, 2025.  Question: Is there a separate quota for wards of AAI employees? Answer: Yes, there is a separate reserved quota for the wards of Airports Authority of India employees. A total of 20 positions are there for wards of AAI.  Question: What is the AAI Sports Contract Scheme and how is it different from the AAI Sports Scholarship Scheme? Answer: The AAI Sports Scholarship Scheme is a contractual scheme, whereas the AAI Sports Scholarship Scheme for Outstanding Sportspersons 2025-26 aims to award promising young players.   Question: Can the AAI withdraw the scholarship? Answer: Yes, AAI reserves the right to withdraw the scholarship at any time without prior notice or assigning any reason.

Name: CENTRAL SANSKRIT UNIVERSITY (CSU) MERIT SCHOLARSHIP 2025-26
Award: A monthly scholarship of up to ₹2,500 and other benefits
Eligibility: Students studying Sanskrit/Pali/Prakrit as a main or optional subject
About: Central Sanskrit University (CSU) Merit Scholarship 2025-26 is an opportunity provided under the Central Schemes of Ministry of Education, Government of India, to students pursuing studies of the Sanskrit/Pali/Prakrit language as a main or optional subject. The scholarship aims to encourage and promote the use of Sanskrit. Selected students will receive a monthly scholarship of up to ₹2,500 and other benefits.  Source: Central Sanskrit University
Eligibility_Detail: Students studying Sanskrit/Pali/Prakrit as a main or optional subject
Benefits: Selected candidates will receive the benefits as follows:-  Course	Amount Class 9/Purva-Madhyama-1st year/10/Purva-Madhyama-2nd year or equivalent course	₹500 per month for 10 months Class 11/12/Prak-Shastri-1st year/Uttarmadhyama-1st year/PrakShastri-2nd /Uttarmadhyama-2nd year or equivalent course	₹600 per month for 10 months Shastri-1st/2nd/3rd/B.A.1st/2nd/3rd year/B.A.(Hons) 1st/2nd/3rd year or equivalent course	₹800 per month for 10 months Acharya-1st/2nd/M.A.-1st/2nd year or equivalent in Sanskrit/Pali/Prakrit	₹1,000 per month for 10 months Vidyavaridhi/Ph.D in Sanskrit/Pali/Prakrit	₹2,500 per month for 36 months  Note:-  Ph.D./Vidyavaridhi candidates will receive an additional contingency amount of ₹5,000 per year. The scholarship for Ph.D./Vidyavaridhi candidates will be provided for the full duration of 3 years (36 months), subject to satisfactory performance. The candidate must send a progress report and utilisation certificate to the guide and head of the department for scholarship renewal for the upcoming academic years.
Documents: Scanned copy of photograph and signature of the applicant (Size - Maximum 50 KB)  Note:- Related details of the applicants will be verified by the Principal/HOD/Dean and countersigned by a respective authority mentioned in section 'F' in the ‘Scholarship Details’ document.
How_to_Apply: Eligible candidates can apply following these steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below and read all the details. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and fill in the required details to register. (Note:- If already registered, log in using the registered email ID.) Step 3: Navigate to the ‘Apply for Scholarship by the Student’ and click on the ‘Student Login for Online Scholarship Application’ tab. Step 4: Click on ‘Register’ and use the appropriate information to register on the website. Step 5: After successful registration, use the appropriate credentials to log in. Step 6: Click on ‘Online Application for Scholarship’, then fill in the required information, upload the necessary documents and submit the application.
Important_Dates: Application Deadline: 30th September 2025
Selection_Criteria: Candidates will be selected by a scholarship committee based on their merit and fulfilment of the eligibility criteria. Preference will be given first to traditional/oriental students, and then to the honours and M.A. students.
Terms_and_Conditions: The scholarship will be disbursed to the candidate’s bank account by Public Finance Management System (PFMS)/ National Electronic Fund Transfer (NEFT)/ Reserve Bank of India (RBI)/ Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT)/ E-transfer. The scholarship amount for 10 months will be provided starting from 1st July and ending on 30th April. Students have to apply every year for the scholarship. Applicants should have an Aadhar-linked bank account in a nationalised bank system to receive the scholarship. For students up to class 12, a joint bank account is allowed. The applicant must not be receiving any other funding during the scholarship period. Undertaking a job or course of study unrelated to the component of Sanskrit will result in disqualification from the scholarship. If the applicant is awarded a scholarship from any other source, they must inform the university immediately. The applicants must not have a gap year in their studies to receive the scholarship. The university can modify the rules of the scholarship and reject any application without stating the reason. Merit Certificates will be provided to those students who voluntarily return the scholarships and the scholarship may be given to needy pupils. If the student is selected for the award of the scholarship but he/she wants to give his/her scholarship to the poor/needy student, the appreciation certificate of such student will be issued instead of the scholarship amount. FAQs: Q: What is the selection process for this scholarship award? | A: The selection of scholars for the IET India Scholarship Award 2025 will take place in four stages. The details of each stage are provided below – Stage 1: Online Application & Evaluation Only those candidates who clear the eligibility criteria as set out by the IET India Scholarship Award will be considered in the Online Application round. Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an online test on STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) essentials. The shortlisted candidates from Stage 1 will make it to Stage 2 and be included in the top 10 percentile of applicants from every region (North, East & Northeast, South 1, South 2, and West). For the South region, the division of states will be: South 1: Karnataka, Telangana, and Kerala. South 2: Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and Pondicherry. The scores will be calculated from the total applications received, based on the weightages assigned to each of the questions in the online application form mentioned below: Weightages considered for the online application Academics – 52% Extra & Co-Curricular – 20% Professional – 13% (Membership in professional bodies, coordinating activities of the professional bodies, office bearer of any professional body/student chapter, contributing to professional bodies through paper presentations etc.) Outreach – 10% (Activities other than institutional activities like coordinating or organising industrial visits, organising lectures/seminars, etc.) IET Student Membership – 15% Stage 2: Online test Shortlisted applicants from Stage 1 will be invited to take an All-India online test. This online test is conducted to assess the shortlisted applicant’s knowledge in core engineering subjects: Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Mechanical, Electrical, Electronics, and Computer Science. There will be a total of approximately 50 multiple-choice questions. The top scoring 30 applicants in each Region (North, South 1 and South 2, East & Northeast and West) will progress to Stage 3. Please note that ONLY the applicants selected for the next round, that is, the regional presentation round, will be contacted further and receive an email from IET India by 15th July 2025. Stage 3: Regional Rounds In the Regional Level Assessment, applicants shortlisted from each region will present on topics assigned by the jury members. Five major societal challenges will be identified, and students will be invited to propose technological solutions for them. Each region’s challenge topic will be based on current and emerging issues specific to that region. The topic will be communicated to the contestants at least 10 days in advance to allow adequate preparation. This round will take place in person on the scheduled presentation date in a central city for each region. Venue details and timings will be shared with the shortlisted students in advance. Each Regional jury panel will identify and felicitate a Winner and a Runner-up, both of whom will receive prizes. Only the Winner will move on to the National Finals. The Regional Jury members will guide and mentor the winners for the next round. The Regional Assessment will be conducted across 5 regions: North Region - Delhi East & North  East Region - Kolkata West Region - Mumbai South 1 Region - Bangalore South 2 Region - Chennai Stage 4: The National Finals The National Finals of the IET India Scholarship Awards 2025 will be held on 15th September 2025. The five winners from the Regional Rounds will present their technological solutions and their vision for them, based on the engineering problem chosen in the previous round. These National Finalists will be evaluated based on their presentations to the national jury and their overall growth throughout their participation in each stage of the Scholarship Awards. Applicants will be assessed on their performance and the improvement demonstrated in each round. The National Finals may also include a Group Discussion, with the topic provided by the jury on the day of the event. The National Finals will be held in Bangalore. Name: AICTE SAMARTHAN INTERNSHIP CONNECT 2025
Award: Certificate of appreciation and other benefits
Eligibility: UG and PG students
About: The AICTE SAMARTHAN Internship Connect 2025 is an opportunity provided by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), to Undergraduate and Postgraduate students of Jammu, Kashmir, Ladakh, Andaman and Nicobar Islands & North Eastern states. This is a 15-day internship that aims to expose students from these regions to cutting-edge research in the fields of science and technology at institutions of higher learning. The selected candidates shall receive a certificate of appreciation and other benefits.
Eligibility_Detail: UG and PG students
Benefits: The selected candidates will receive:-  Free food and accommodation Second class sleeper to and fro expenses Certificate of Internship Completion by the host institution
Documents: Aadhaar Card All academic marksheets and certificates Bona-fide certificate from head of the institution
How_to_Apply: Eligible candidates can follow these steps to apply:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.  Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Click on ‘Student Register’ on the right-hand side of the page. Step 4: Fill in all the details and click on the ‘Submit’ button. Step 5: Once your registration is authenticated by the TPO (Training and Placement Officer) or Nodal Officer, you can proceed with the application.   Note:- Students can register themselves only after the Nodal Officer registers on the portal with the Nomination Letter and the AICTE verifies the nomination.
Important_Dates: Application Deadline: 10th May 2025
Selection_Criteria: Applicants pursuing an undergraduate degree will be selected on the basis of their UG and Class 12 scores. Postgraduate students will be selected on the basis of their PG and UG scores.  Note:- If candidates have the same scores, the scores from their immediately preceding grade will be used to determine the rank. If those scores are also identical, the older candidate, based on their date of birth in their Aadhaar card, will be ranked higher.
Terms_and_Conditions: Only students whose applications are verified by the designated nodal officers at their respective institutions will be eligible for the internship. Undergraduate students can undertake a maximum of three internships throughout their program, while postgraduate students are limited to two. Allotment letters will be issued by email to the individual email IDs of the students upon successful selection. The final allotment list will be determined by the prepared rank list, the order of institute preferences submitted by students, and the number of available seats in each institute. Students who fail to respond to their first-round allotment within the specified timeframe will be automatically rejected, and those seats will be offered to other students in the second round.  Note:- Any representations/grievances will not be entertained in cases of non-response from candidates in allotted seats.
Contact_Details: All India Council for Technical Education G5P3+8PH,  Nelson Mandela Marg, Vasant Kunj,  New Delhi, Delhi 110070 Email ID: helpdesk.tlb@aicte-india.org  | Phone Number: +91-11-2958-1303, +91-11-2958-1303  Source: https://www.aicte.gov.in/

Award: An annual fellowship of up to ₹7,50,000
Eligibility: Indian Ph.D. degree holders
About: The Young Faculty Research Fellowship 2025 is an opportunity provided by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology to Indian researchers with a PhD degree. It aims to encourage specialised research in emerging areas of electronics and IT. The selected candidates can receive a fellowship of up to ₹7,50,00 per annum.

Name: SASAKAWA INDIA LEPROSY FOUNDATION NURSING SCHOLARSHIP 2025-26
Eligibility: Students residing in self-settled leprosy colonies & pursuing nursing studies in India
About: Sasakawa India Leprosy Foundation Nursing Scholarship 2025-26 is an opportunity provided by the Sasakawa-India Leprosy Foundation (S-ILF) for B.Sc. Nursing (Basic) & GNM students residing in self-settled leprosy colonies. The selected students will receive financial support for education.
Eligibility_Detail: Students residing in self-settled leprosy colonies & pursuing nursing studies in India
Benefits: The selected students will receive monthly/annual financial support.
Documents: Photocopies of: Passport-size photographs of the applicant Birth certificate or other proof of date of birth Marksheets of Class 10 and 12 board exams Leprosy certificate of parent(s) which may include a treatment certificate, District Leprosy Officer certificate, or Disability certificate mentioning Hansen's disease/leprosy Admission letter/call letter issued by the institute the applicant is applying to Proof of admission in a government-recognised institute (only required if the student has already secured admission)  Note:- Applicants who haven't secured admission yet must submit Annexure-I (available on the S-ILF website).
How_to_Apply: Eligible candidates can apply for the scholarship using the steps listed below:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button. Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and enter the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID) Step 3: Click the respective option for Nursing Scholarship and access the registration link. Step 4: Fill in the details and click 'Register'. Step 5: Fill in the required details, attach the relevant documents, and submit the application.
Important_Dates: Application Deadline: 30 May 2024
Selection_Criteria: Eligible candidates will be shortlisted based on their marks in the last board examination. Shortlisted students will be called for a personal interview. The selection committee will then select the top candidates for the scholarship. If a selected candidate declines the scholarship, the offer will be extended to the next eligible candidate on the waiting list.
Terms_and_Conditions: No capitation fee will be paid for admission.  Incomplete applications will be disqualified. Regular attendance, dedication to learning, and high academic performance are expected from scholarship recipients.  Applicants receiving scholarships from other organisations are ineligible for this program. Application forms can also be obtained from State Leaders of the Association of People Affected with Leprosy (APAL). Name: AHIMSA FELLOWSHIP 2025
Award: A monthly stipend of up to ₹20,000 and other benefits
Eligibility: Bachelor's degree holders
About: The Ahimsa Fellowship 2025 is an 8-month full-time opportunity for graduates designed to create a network of effective animal protection leaders. Through this programme, fellows will gain the skills and knowledge to collaborate closely with state governments and district administrations to enforce animal protection laws in India. It seeks trained professionals who can capably assist the Government in fulfilling its statutory duties through systemic apparatuses like State Animal Welfare Boards, Societies For The Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, Infirmaries, Slaughterhouse Monitoring Committees, Animal Birth Control Monitoring Committees etc. Successful applicants will receive a competitive monthly stipend of up to ₹20,000 along with other valuable benefits.  The fellowship programme is powered by the People For Animals Public Policy Foundation and People For Animals Uttarakhand. It is supported by Ahimsa Trust, Humane Society International – India, Mercy For Animals – India, Fish Welfare Initiative India, India Animal Fund, Kaivalya Education Foundation, Miranda House College (University of Delhi), and NALSAR University of Law.
Eligibility_Detail: Bachelor's degree holders
Benefits: For the first two months of the 8-month program, fellows will receive a stipend of ₹10,000 per month. Thereafter, a monthly stipend of ₹20,000.  The stipend is designed to cover expenses related to completing program assignments. Travel and accommodation for the residential contact classes will be covered by Team Ahimsa Fellowship and is NOT to be borne by the candidate. Out-of-pocket expenses beyond the stipend may be reimbursed on a case-by-case basis.

Name: SAMSUNG SOLVE FOR TOMORROW INDIA 2025
Award: Grants up to ₹25,00,000 and other benefits
Eligibility: Indian students aged between 14-22 years
About: Samsung Solve for Tomorrow India 2025 is an opportunity offered by Samsung India to students aged between 14 and 22 years. This year's program is a strategic partnership between Samsung India, the United Nations in India, IIT Delhi, the Foundation for Innovation & Technology Transfer (FITT), and the Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology. It aims to equip students with stronger problem-solving and creative thinking skills.  The competition is divided into two tracks:  School Track (solving for India): It is open for 14-17-year-olds under the Theme "Community and Inclusion." It aims to empower underprivileged groups, improve accessibility to healthcare, and promote social inclusion for all. Youth Track (solving for the world): It is open for 18-22-year-olds under the Theme "Environment and Sustainability." It aims to reduce carbon footprint, protect the environment, and promote sustainability.  Selected individuals will receive grants up to ₹25,00,000 along with additional benefits.
Eligibility_Detail: Indian students aged between 14-22 years
Benefits: Selected team/individual will receive the benefits as follow:  For School Track:  Grant of ₹20,000 for prototype development per team for the top 10 teams shortlisted in phase 2 of the program Grant of ₹1,00,000 for prototype enhancement per team to be given to the top 5 teams shortlisted in phase 3 of the program Grant of ₹25,00,000 for prototype advancement for the winning team selected in phase 4 of the program  For Youth Track:  Grant of ₹20,000 for prototype development per team for the top 10 teams shortlisted in phase 2 of the program Grant of ₹1,00,000 for prototype enhancement per team to be given to the top 5 teams shortlisted in phase 3 of the program Grant of ₹50,00,000 awarded as a seed grant and incubation grant for the winning team selected in phase 4 of the program Goodwill Award: The highest-scoring finalist according to audience polling will receive ₹1,00,000. Social Media Champion Award: The highest-scoring finalist team according to social engagement will receive ₹50,000.  Note:-  The 10 semi-finalist teams will receive hands-on training from a range of industry professionals, including Samsung, MeitY, IIT-Delhi, and technical assistance from the United Nations in India. Participants will also get milestone-based grants for prototype development and improvement, a chance to attend a curated innovation walk with targeted interactions with Samsung officials, and private mentorship and coaching to help them turn their ideas into prototypes. The 10 Semi-Finalist and 5 Finalist Teams will receive a selection of Samsung products. The winning team's school/college will receive Samsung products to enhance their educational quality and development.
Documents: A complete application form
How_to_Apply: Eligible applicants can apply using the following steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button. Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID.) Step 3: Read the details carefully and navigate to the 'Apply Now' button at the top right-hand side of the dashboard.  Step 4: Select the age range, enter the email address along with the displayed CAPTCHA, and click 'Submit' to receive a one-time passcode (OTP). Step 6: Enter the OTP received on your email and the given captcha, then click ‘Login’ button. Step 5: Fill in the required information, upload the necessary documents and click the ‘Confirm’ button.
Important_Dates: Application Last Date: 30 May 2025
Selection_Criteria: Applicants' submissions will undergo a rigorous multi-stage review process to identify the most promising solutions.  Applications will be screened by subject matter experts from the Foundation of Innovation & Technology Transfer (FITT) - IIT Delhi based on their relevance to a social problem, technical feasibility, market potential, and team competence. The ideas will be evaluated using the following criteria:  Significance of the problem statement and its impact on society • Proposed solution's novelty and viability • Qualifications of the group or individual • Plan for commercialisation  Applicants' submissions will be reviewed by a jury comprising industry veterans from Samsung during the second and third rounds.
Terms_and_Conditions: Parents or legal guardians may register applicants between 14 and 17 years old on their behalf. Applications with incomplete information or missing documents will be rejected.  Applicants can participate once, either independently or as a member of a single team. Each applicant/team can submit a single idea only. Applicants must submit original work, free from plagiarism of text, ideas, images, audio, or video recordings from third parties. Shortlisted participants must attend all workshops, mentoring sessions, regional pitch events, and the Final Pitch Event as instructed by the Implementation Partner. Failure to attend any of these activities may result in disqualification at Samsung's discretion. All applicants must act in good faith, comply with all requests from Samsung or its implementation partner, and avoid any actions that could damage Samsung's reputation or endanger the health, safety, or well-being of other applicants or employees. Failure to comply with these conditions may result in Samsung rejecting the application. Samsung's decisions regarding the competition and all related matters will be final and binding on applicants and participants. Any disputes arising from the competition will be governed by the laws of India. These terms and conditions shall be construed and interpreted in accordance with the exclusive laws of India, and the courts located at New Delhi shall have exclusive jurisdiction over all subject matters related to the competition. The applicant/participant acknowledges and agrees that Samsung and its authorised partners may collect, use, disclose, transfer, and store their personally identifiable data/information in accordance with Samsung's privacy policy. The applicant/participant are advised to read the privacy policy carefully.  Note:- For more details, kindly refer to the 'Contest Detail Document'.
Contact_Details: Samsung India 6th Floor, DLF Centre Sansad Marg, New Delhi-110001 Email ID: solvefortomorrow@samsung.com | Phone number: (1800) 5726 7864 Source: samsung.com
Important_Links: Media Link Latest scholarship link Apply online link Original website
FAQs: Question: What is the last date to apply for the Samsung Solve for Tomorrow India 2025? Answer: The last date to apply for this opportunity is 30th June 2025.   Question: Who is offering this scheme? Answer: The Samsung India Organisation is offering this opportunity.  Question: What are the awards for this scheme? Answer: The selected individuals will receive grants up to ₹25,00,000 along with additional benefits.  Question: Which age group candidates are eligible to apply for this scheme? Answer: To be eligible to apply, students must be between 14 and 22 years of age.   Question: Can Indian citizens apply for this scheme? Answer: Yes, Indian citizens are eligible to apply for this scheme.

FAQs: Q: What is the selection process for this scholarship award? | A: The selection of scholars for the IET India Scholarship Award 2025 will take place in four stages. The details of each stage are provided below – Stage 1: Online Application & Evaluation Only those candidates who clear the eligibility criteria as set out by the IET India Scholarship Award will be considered in the Online Application round. Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an online test on STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) essentials. The shortlisted candidates from Stage 1 will make it to Stage 2 and be included in the top 10 percentile of applicants from every region (North, East & Northeast, South 1, South 2, and West). For the South region, the division of states will be: South 1: Karnataka, Telangana, and Kerala. South 2: Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and Pondicherry. The scores will be calculated from the total applications received, based on the weightages assigned to each of the questions in the online application form mentioned below: Weightages considered for the online application Academics – 52% Extra & Co-Curricular – 20% Professional – 13% (Membership in professional bodies, coordinating activities of the professional bodies, office bearer of any professional body/student chapter, contributing to professional bodies through paper presentations etc.) Outreach – 10% (Activities other than institutional activities like coordinating or organising industrial visits, organising lectures/seminars, etc.) IET Student Membership – 15% Stage 2: Online test Shortlisted applicants from Stage 1 will be invited to take an All-India online test. This online test is conducted to assess the shortlisted applicant’s knowledge in core engineering subjects: Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Mechanical, Electrical, Electronics, and Computer Science. There will be a total of approximately 50 multiple-choice questions. The top scoring 30 applicants in each Region (North, South 1 and South 2, East & Northeast and West) will progress to Stage 3. Please note that ONLY the applicants selected for the next round, that is, the regional presentation round, will be contacted further and receive an email from IET India by 15th July 2025. Stage 3: Regional Rounds In the Regional Level Assessment, applicants shortlisted from each region will present on topics assigned by the jury members. Five major societal challenges will be identified, and students will be invited to propose technological solutions for them. Each region’s challenge topic will be based on current and emerging issues specific to that region. The topic will be communicated to the contestants at least 10 days in advance to allow adequate preparation. This round will take place in person on the scheduled presentation date in a central city for each region. Venue details and timings will be shared with the shortlisted students in advance. Each Regional jury panel will identify and felicitate a Winner and a Runner-up, both of whom will receive prizes. Only the Winner will move on to the National Finals. The Regional Jury members will guide and mentor the winners for the next round. The Regional Assessment will be conducted across 5 regions: North Region - Delhi East & North  East Region - Kolkata West Region - Mumbai South 1 Region - Bangalore South 2 Region - Chennai Stage 4: The National Finals The National Finals of the IET India Scholarship Awards 2025 will be held on 15th September 2025. The five winners from the Regional Rounds will present their technological solutions and their vision for them, based on the engineering problem chosen in the previous round. These National Finalists will be evaluated based on their presentations to the national jury and their overall growth throughout their participation in each stage of the Scholarship Awards. Applicants will be assessed on their performance and the improvement demonstrated in each round. The National Finals may also include a Group Discussion, with the topic provided by the jury on the day of the event. The National Finals will be held in Bangalore. Venue details and timings will be communicated to the shortlisted students in advance. || Q: Is there any age limit to apply for this scholarship award? | A: The National Finals of the IET India Scholarship Award 2025 will be held on September 15, 2025 || Q: Is there any age limit to apply for this scholarship award? | A: The National Finals of the IET India Scholarship Award 2025 will be held on September 15, 2025 || Q: What are the subjects/syllabus of the online test? | A: There is no age limit for the applicant to apply for the IET India Scholarship Award. || Q: What are the dates for the online test? | A: Questions will be asked from the following 7 subjects: Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Mechanical, Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science. || Q: Am I required to pay any charges for the scholarship program? | A: The online test will be conducted from July 2-4, 2025. || Q: What happens after the online test? | A: No, you are not required to make any payments for the online test. || Q: What happens after the online test? | A: No, you are not required to make any payments for the online test.

Name: MIRAE ASSET SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM FOR UNDERGRADUATE STUDENTS
Eligibility: For UG students
About: The Mirae Asset Foundation Scholarship Program, an initiative of the Mirae Asset Foundation, aims to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from underprivileged sections of society across India. Under this scholarship, students currently pursuing undergraduate or postgraduate courses in the country can apply and stand a chance to receive a scholarship of up to INR 50,000.  About Mirae Asset Foundation  Mirae Asset Foundation, established in 2018, is the Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) arm of Mirae Asset Financial Group in India. Guided by the vision of its parent organisation, the Mirae Asset Park Hyeon Joo Foundation in Korea, the Foundation is dedicated to advancing education at all levels. Its initiatives include providing scholarships to students and collaborating with schools, colleges, government agencies, civil society organisations, and other partners to deliver impactful educational programs across the country.
Eligibility_Detail: Open to students who are currently pursuing postgraduate courses in India.  Students must have scored at least 60% in the previous academic year.  The annual family income of the applicants should be less than INR 8,00,000 per annum from all sources.  Students from PAN India can apply for the scholarship.  Children of Buddy4Study employees are not eligible to participate.   Note: The total academic fees paid to the respective institution should not be less than INR 50,000.

Name: CHIEF MINISTER FELLOWSHIP PROGRAM, MAHARASHTRA 2025
Award: A monthly stipend of ₹61,500 and a certificate
Eligibility: Graduates between the age of 21 to 26 years
About: The Chief Minister Fellowship (CMF) Program, Maharashtra 2025 is an opportunity provided by the The Government of Maharashtra, India, to graduates between the age of 21 to 26 years. This 12 month internship provides the youth with an opportunity to be a part of the government and help the administration with their innovative ideas. The selected candidates shall receive a monthly stipend of ₹61,500 and a certificate of appreciation.
Eligibility_Detail: Graduates between the age of 21 to 26 years
Benefits: The selected candidates will receive:-  A monthly stipend of ₹56,100 and travel expenses of ₹5,400 which amounts to ₹61,500 per month.  Certificate of appreciation. Accidental insurance cover during the period of the internship.
Documents: Applicants shall possess the original copy and the photocopy of their:  Identity proof Academic marksheets Experience letters
How_to_Apply: Eligible candidates can follow these steps to apply:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.  Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Click on ‘PROCEED FOR APPLICATION/SIGN IN’ on the upper right hand side of the page. Step 4: Scroll down on the page that opens up and click on the blue colour ‘Proceed for application/Sign in’ tab. Step 5: Click on ‘New user? Register’ on the upper left hand side of the page.  Step 6: Read the important instructions carefully, tick the check box and click on the OK button.  Step 7: Fill in all the necessary details to register yourself.  Step 8: Log in via the user ID and password provided on your email and proceed with your application.
Important_Dates: Application Deadline: 05th May 2025
Selection_Criteria: The applicants will have to go through 2 stages of selection: Stage 1: This consists of an online MCQ test of 10 marks. On the basis of the marks secured, a total of 210 applicants are selected for the second stage. Stage 2: The selected candidates have to submit the assigned essays and appear for an offline interview. Based on their performance, a list of 60 selected candidates shall be published.  (Note:- The essay can be in Marathi, Hindi, or English.)
Terms_and_Conditions: Selected candidates are entitled for 8 days of leave during the period of the internship.      Travel and other related expenses for the offline interview shall not be reimbursed.  The date and time of the interview shall be conveyed to the candidates via email.
Contact_Details: Directorate of Economics and Statistics. Planning Department, Government of Maharashtra. Administrative Building, 8th floor, Bandra (East), Mumbai-400051 Email ID: cmfellowship-mah@gov.in | Phone Number: 8411960005 (Mon - Sat, 10am to 5pm) Source: https://mahades.maharashtra.gov.in/

Name: NIIT UNIVERSITY SCHOLARSHIP-CUM-ADMISSION SEAT PROGRAMME 2025-26
Award: Scholarship of up to 100% tuition fee waiver
Eligibility: Class 12 passed students who wish to take admission at NIIT University
About: NIIT University (NU) invites applications for its prestigious Scholarship-cum-Admission Seat Programme for the academic year 2025-26. This initiative is designed to empower meritorious and deserving students across India by offering up to 100% tuition fee waiver and a streamlined admission opportunity into any of the NU’s flagship undergraduate programmes mentioned below:  B.Tech. (Bachelor of Technology) in Biotechnology, Computer Science & Engineering, Artificial Intelligence and Data Science, Electronics & Communication Engineering, Cyber Security, and IoT & Automation 3-year BBA (Bachelor of Business Administration) Dual Degree Integrated MBA (Post-Class 12)  About NIIT University (NU)   Established in 2009 by Shri Rajendra S. Pawar and Shri Vijay K. Thadani, Founders of the NIIT Group, NIIT University (NU) was envisioned as a model institution committed to delivering industry-aligned, technology-driven, and research-focused education.   NU aims to offer Indian students a globally relevant education rooted in Indian values. As a globally benchmarked, multi-disciplinary university, it sets
Eligibility_Detail: Meritorious students aspiring to pursue undergraduate education at NU in the following programmes for the academic session 2025-26:  B.Tech (CSE, AI & DS, ECE, Cyber Security, Biotechnology, IoT & Automation) BBA (3-year programme) Dual Degree Integrated MBA (5-year programme) Academic requirements for admission -  For B.Tech: Minimum 55% aggregate in both Classes 10 & 12 (PCM/PCB/PCMB) For BBA/Dual Degree Integrated MBA: Minimum 50% aggregate in both Classes 10 & 12 (any stream) Applicants who demonstrate exceptional academic merit will be considered for the merit scholarships offered by the University A valid rank/score in one of the following entrance exams: JEE-Main, BITSAT, SAT, CUET, IPMAT, or any recognized State Engineering Entrance Examination.  NOTE:   Candidates without a valid entrance exam score must appear for the NIIT University Aptitude Test (NUAT). For applicants from IB or Cambridge boards, a minimum of Grade 4 (IB Board) or Grade C (Cambridge IGCSE Board) is required. Selection will be based on a composite assessment of the applicant’s performance in: Class 10 and Class 12 board examinations Entrance exam score Personal interaction Each entrance exam and education board (State/Central/International) will be evaluated separately to ensure fairness in academic assessment.  Preference will be given to applicants from Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) with a family income below INR 8 lakh per annum.
Benefits: Up to 100% tuition fee waiver for the entire duration of the course:  B.Tech – up to 4 years BBA – up to 3 years Dual Degree Integrated MBA – up to 5 years  The scholarship amount will be determined based on academic merit, entrance exam performance, and eligibility criteria.  Note: The scholarship is renewable annually and will continue only if the student meets the requisite academic performance criteria as defined by the NIIT University.
